title: Well-Stocked Products
description: You are given three tables: product categories, products, and inventory records per warehouse. Find names of products with total stock across all warehouses of at least 100, ordered alphabetically.
inputFormat:
categories(id, name)
products(id, name, category_id, unit_price)
inventory(id, product_id, warehouse, stock)
outputFormat: One product name per line, ordered alphabetically.
constraints: Use JOIN and GROUP BY. Use HAVING SUM(stock) >= 100. Include ORDER BY name. Only SELECT queries are allowed.
sampleSolution: SELECT p.name FROM products p JOIN inventory i ON p.id = i.product_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name HAVING SUM(i.stock) >= 100 ORDER BY p.name;
hint: Join products with inventory, GROUP BY product, and filter HAVING SUM(i.stock) >= 100.

testCases.setupSql: CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, unit_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE inventory (id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, warehouse TEXT, stock INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Electronics'),(2,'Clothing'),(3,'Food'); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Laptop',1,800),(2,'T-Shirt',2,30),(3,'Coffee',3,10),(4,'Phone',1,600); INSERT INTO inventory VALUES (1,1,'Warehouse A',40),(2,1,'Warehouse B',70),(3,2,'Warehouse A',50),(4,2,'Warehouse B',30),(5,3,'Warehouse A',200),(6,4,'Warehouse A',20);

CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, unit_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE inventory (id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, warehouse TEXT, stock INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Tools'),(2,'Garden'),(3,'Sports'); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Hammer',1,15),(2,'Shovel',2,25),(3,'Bicycle',3,400),(4,'Rake',2,20); INSERT INTO inventory VALUES (1,1,'North',60),(2,1,'South',50),(3,2,'North',30),(4,3,'East',200),(5,4,'North',80),(6,4,'South',40);

CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, unit_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE inventory (id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, warehouse TEXT, stock INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Luxury'); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Diamond Ring',1,5000),(2,'Gold Watch',1,3000); INSERT INTO inventory VALUES (1,1,'Vault',5),(2,2,'Vault',3);

CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, unit_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE inventory (id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, warehouse TEXT, stock INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Beverages'); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Water',1,1),(2,'Juice',1,2),(3,'Soda',1,1); INSERT INTO inventory VALUES (1,1,'WH1',200),(2,1,'WH2',100),(3,2,'WH1',150),(4,3,'WH1',50),(5,3,'WH2',60);

CREATE TABLE categories (id INTEGER, name TEXT); CREATE TABLE products (id INTEGER, name TEXT, category_id INTEGER, unit_price INTEGER); CREATE TABLE inventory (id INTEGER, product_id INTEGER, warehouse TEXT, stock INTEGER); INSERT INTO categories VALUES (1,'Stationery'); INSERT INTO products VALUES (1,'Pen',1,1),(2,'Notebook',1,5); INSERT INTO inventory VALUES (1,1,'A',60),(2,1,'B',40),(3,2,'A',99);
